overall_summary: Excellent lap — you are ahead of the reference. Current gap: 9.657s faster than reference. The biggest opportunity is Sector 3 (+287.121s), where 0.4 km/h is being left on the table through the corners. These corrections alone are worth 1.30s. That is a significant step forward.

priority_actions.issue: Entry speed is 90.0 km/h below reference but apex speed is acceptable — over-braking on entry.
priority_actions.instruction: Trail the brakes into the corner rather than releasing fully at turn-in. Carry more entry speed and use trail braking to rotate the car to the apex.

priority_actions.issue: Apex speed is 13.4 km/h below reference (81.0 vs 94.4 km/h). Significant corner exit speed deficit.
priority_actions.instruction: Despite a late brake point, apex speed is below reference — car not rotating. Use higher initial brake pressure to pitch the car in, trail off through apex. Hit 94 km/h and the exit speed takes care of itself.
priority_actions.evidence: Apex: 81.0 km/h vs 94.4 km/h reference (-13.4 km/h).

priority_actions.issue: Braking 10m too early compared to reference.
priority_actions.instruction: Move the brake marker 10m later. Reference brakes at 1335m, driver at 1325m.

sector_feedback.headline: Minimum corner speed is 13.4 km/h below reference — driver is over-slowing significantly through this sector.
sector_feedback.details: Average throttle application is 15% lower than reference — driver is hesitant getting back on power.

sector_feedback.details: Corner speed is 4.0 km/h above reference — good commitment through the bends.
